title: Patrick Stickles Directed Eve 6 & Fastball’s Funny Tour Promo Video
author: Chris DeVille
summary:
'90s pop-alternative fixtures Eve 6 and Fastball are going on tour together, and they made a funny video to promote it. More specifically, Patrick Stickles made the video. The Titus Andronicus frontman — whose fans are definitely devoted, no matter what Ian Cohen says — directed the lo-fi clip, which emphasizes the value of an Eve 6/Fastball co-bill. Whereas many '90s nostalgia tours will only afford you two or three hits, this one presents you with six. See for yourself:

The post Patrick Stickles Directed Eve 6 & Fastball’s Funny Tour Promo Video appeared first on Stereogum.
full_text:
'90s pop-alternative fixtures Eve 6 and Fastball are going on tour together, and they made a funny video to promote it. More specifically, Patrick Stickles made the video. The Titus Andronicus frontman — whose fans are definitely devoted, no matter what Ian Cohen says — directed the lo-fi clip, which emphasizes the value of an Eve 6/Fastball co-bill. Whereas many '90s nostalgia tours will only afford you two or three hits, this one presents you with six. See for yourself:

Stickles, who wrote a great Lou Reed retrospective for Stereogum back in the early COVID days, has been directing these kinds of videos for Eve 6 for a minute. Last year, he and Max Collins teamed up on this one when Eve 6 and Titus toured together:

Eve 6 and Titus go even farther back. Check out Stickles guesting on "Promise" in 2022:

Stickles would like you to know that he can manifest promotional social media content for you as well, seeing as the business of indie rock ain't paying the bills:

Imagine what this guy could do with a real budget! C'mon, patrons of the arts!
